meta.description: Structural definition for pro fight offers — not actual fight data. Actual offers are generated by the promoter system and delivered via inbox. The engine reads this when constructing offers to ensure all required fields are present. The UI reads this to know how to render any offer type correctly. Clauses are attached to specific offers — the player sees them clearly before accepting or declining.

cardPositions.label: Main Event
cardPositions.description: Top of the card. Full arena spotlight. The headline fight that sells the show.

cardPositions.label: Co-Main Event
cardPositions.description: Second billing. Significant exposure — often a title fight or high-profile bout.

cardPositions.label: Featured Bout
cardPositions.description: Third-billing fight. Solid exposure for rising contenders and regional champions.

cardPositions.label: Undercard
cardPositions.description: Scheduled before the main card. Good professional experience, limited public exposure.

cardPositions.label: Preliminary Bout
cardPositions.description: Prelim cards. Development fights for early-career professionals. Minimum purse, maximum ring time.

clauseTypes.label: Rematch Clause
clauseTypes.description: The loser is guaranteed a rematch within a defined window if they held a title going in. Protects both the promoter's investment and the fighter's title position.

clauseTypes.label: Exclusivity Clause
clauseTypes.description: The fighter cannot accept bouts from competing promoters for the duration specified. Locks the fighter to this promoter's cards.

clauseTypes.label: Weight Clause
clauseTypes.description: A financial penalty is applied if the fighter misses weight. Protects the opponent and promoter from late weight miss disruption.

clauseTypes.label: Step-Aside Agreement
clauseTypes.description: A mandatory challenger agrees to step aside and allow another fight to happen in exchange for a financial payment and a future guaranteed title shot.

clauseTypes.label: Purse Split
clauseTypes.description: Defines the percentage split of the combined fight purse between the two fighters. Non-standard splits can be negotiated by powerful promoters or demanded by a champion.
